summary: Comprehensive testing of Phases 1-5 inventory system overhaul. All 16 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified: Category Management tab with type badges, Transfer Item dialog (UNIQUE vs NON-UNIQUE flows), Report Damage/Lost dialogs, SSD Transfer with 'Reason for Transfer' dropdown, Data Offload page.
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ GET /api/categories returns categories with type (unique/non_unique) and item_count
✓ PUT /api/categories/{value} updates category label and type
✓ DELETE /api/categories/{value} blocked when items exist in category
✓ POST /api/events/transfer-quantity endpoint for NON-UNIQUE category transfers
✓ POST /api/events/damage-lost-quantity endpoint for NON-UNIQUE category damage/lost
✓ SSD category classified as UNIQUE - requires item selection for transfer
✓ SSD can be marked as 'ready_for_offload' status
✓ Distribution tab excludes damaged/lost items from counts
✓ Categories tab shows all 12 categories with Unique/Quantity-based badges
✓ Transfer dialog: UNIQUE shows 'Select Item ID', NON-UNIQUE shows 'Quantity to Transfer'
✓ SSD Transfer shows 'Reason for SSD Transfer' dropdown with options: SSD Full, Routine Return, Issue/Damage, Other
✓ 'SSD Full' option shows warning: 'This SSD will be marked as Ready for Offload'
✓ Report Damage dialog: UNIQUE shows item select, NON-UNIQUE shows location + quantity
✓ Data Offload page shows SSDs with pending data highlighted
seed_data_creation: Test items created with TEST_ prefix during backend testing (auto-cleaned up)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phases 1-5 inventory overhaul fully verified. Key features: 1) Category CRUD with type badges, 2) Transfer flow differentiates UNIQUE (item select) vs NON-UNIQUE (quantity), 3) Damage/Lost flow differentiates UNIQUE (item select, auto-detect location) vs NON-UNIQUE (location + quantity), 4) SSD Transfer has 'Reason' dropdown with 'SSD Full' marking as ready_for_offload, 5) Data Offload page shows SSDs with pending data.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/categories returns type and item_count
Backend: POST /api/categories creates new category (admin only)
Backend: PUT /api/categories updates category
Backend: DELETE /api/categories blocked with existing items
Backend: DELETE /api/categories succeeds for empty category
Backend: POST /api/events/transfer-quantity endpoint exists
Backend: Quantity transfer works for NON-UNIQUE categories
Backend: UNIQUE categories distinct from NON-UNIQUE
Backend: POST /api/events/damage-lost-quantity endpoint exists
Backend: Damage/Lost endpoint validates status field
Backend: UNIQUE item damage updates status correctly
Backend: SSD category is classified as UNIQUE
Backend: SSD can be marked as ready_for_offload
Backend: GET /api/ssds returns SSD data
Backend: Distribution excludes damaged items
Backend: Distribution excludes lost items
Frontend: Categories tab shows 12 categories with type badges
Frontend: Add Category button visible for admin
Frontend: Transfer Item button opens dialog
Frontend: Transfer dialog shows item select for UNIQUE (SSD)
Frontend: Transfer dialog shows quantity input for NON-UNIQUE (USB Hub)
Frontend: SSD Transfer shows 'Reason for SSD Transfer' dropdown
Frontend: 'SSD Full' option shows ready_for_offload warning
Frontend: Report Damage button opens dialog
Frontend: Report Damage shows item select for UNIQUE (Laptop)
Frontend: Report Damage shows location + quantity for NON-UNIQUE (USB Hub)
Frontend: Data Offload page loads with SSD list
features_verified: Category Management: GET /api/categories returns categories with type and item_count
Delete protection: Cannot delete category with existing items
Categories tab in Inventory page shows all categories with type badges
Transfer Flow (NON-UNIQUE): Only Category + Quantity + From/To (no item select)
Transfer Flow (UNIQUE): Category → Select specific item ID
Damage/Lost (UNIQUE): Select item, location auto-detected from item record
Damage/Lost (NON-UNIQUE): Select category + source location + quantity
SSD Transfer: Shows 'Reason for Transfer' dropdown when category=ssd
SSD Full: When reason is 'ssd_full', SSD marked as 'ready_for_offload'
Data Offload page: Shows SSDs with pending data highlighted
